per_turn_leaks.student_message: Substernal pressure that worsens with exertion — those are hallmarks of cardiac ischemia. In ischemic episodes, the autonomic nervous system often reacts. Have you noticed any sweating during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've definitely been sweating when it happens. It's like my body is working overtime to deal with whatever's going on in there. The sweat can be pretty heavy, actually – not just a little dampness, but really dripping with sweat.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Fast food most days adds dietary risk. Now, with hypertension, diabetes, high cholesterol, a sedentary lifestyle, poor diet, and smoking — that's six cardiac risk factors. Family history is the seventh major one. Has anyone in your family had heart problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father had a heart attack at age 52 and died from it. He was pretty young when it happened, so it's always stuck with me as a concern for my own health.

contradictions.description: Fact states chest tightness is substernal and described as a pressure sensation, while the response indicates it is consistently in the center of the chest and does not radiate.
